meaning_en: To receive or agree
example: She accepted the job offer gladly.

meaning_en: Unfavourable or harmful
example: Adverse weather delayed the match.

meaning_en: Guidance given to someone (noun)
example: The doctor gave him sound advice.

meaning_en: To recommend or counsel (verb)
example: I advise you to study regularly.

meaning_en: To influence (verb)
example: Noise pollution affects concentration badly.

meaning_en: Completely prepared
example: The troops were all ready to march.

meaning_en: To refer to something indirectly
example: The speaker alluded to past failures.

meaning_en: Before the expected time
example: She had already submitted the form.

meaning_en: Sacred table used in religious ceremonies
example: Flowers were placed on the altar.

meaning_en: To make changes to something
example: He altered the plan at the last minute.

meaning_en: Friendly and pleasant (of people)
example: She has an amiable personality.

meaning_en: Done in a friendly spirit (of situations)
example: They reached an amicable settlement.

meaning_en: To promise or give confidence to someone
example: I assure you the results will be fair.

meaning_en: Strongly disinclined
example: She is averse to taking risks.

meaning_en: Uncovered or exposed
example: He walked on bare feet through the mud.

meaning_en: To endure, or a large wild animal
example: She could not bear the pain any longer.

meaning_en: Sleeping place in a train or ship
example: I reserved an upper berth on the train.

meaning_en: Next to or at the side of
example: He sat beside me during the ceremony.

meaning_en: In addition to
example: Besides English, she knows three languages.

meaning_en: The act of being born
example: His birth was celebrated with great joy.

meaning_en: To take something temporarily
example: May I borrow your umbrella please?

meaning_en: Device used to stop a vehicle
example: He pressed the brake hard to avoid a collision.

meaning_en: To separate into pieces by force
example: Do not break the glass vase.

meaning_en: To purchase something
example: I will buy a new book tomorrow.

meaning_en: Near, through, or indicating agent
example: The novel was written by Premchand.

meaning_en: An informal farewell
example: She waved bye and left the room.

meaning_en: Woven fabric or material
example: This cotton cloth is very soft.

meaning_en: To dress or provide clothes to someone
example: She clothed the child in warm layers.

meaning_en: Rough in texture or manner
example: The coarse fabric irritated his skin.

meaning_en: A senior army officer rank
example: The colonel led his regiment into battle.

meaning_en: Something that completes or enhances
example: The sauce perfectly complements the dish.

meaning_en: An expression of praise
example: She received a compliment on her speech.

meaning_en: Frequently recurring with short breaks
example: Continual interruptions broke his concentration.

meaning_en: Without any interruption at all
example: Continuous rain flooded the roads overnight.

meaning_en: An advisory or governing body
example: The municipal council met to discuss roads.

meaning_en: Legal advice or a lawyer
example: He sought the counsel of a senior advocate.

meaning_en: Study programme or direction of movement
example: He enrolled in an engineering course.

meaning_en: Capable of being believed
example: The witness gave a credible account of events.

meaning_hi: भोला / आसानी से विश्वास करने वाला
meaning_en: Too ready to believe things
example: A credulous person is easily cheated.

meaning_en: Dry barren land, or to abandon
example: Camels are well suited to life in the desert.

meaning_en: Sweet course served after a meal
example: We had gulab jamun for dessert.

meaning_en: A machine or tool (noun)
example: This device measures body temperature accurately.

meaning_en: To plan or invent something (verb)
example: She devised a clever strategy to win.

meaning_en: Impartial, having no personal stake
example: A judge must be completely disinterested.

meaning_en: A rough version of a document
example: Submit your first draft before the deadline.

meaning_en: A current of cold air indoors
example: A cold draught entered through the broken window.

meaning_en: To obtain money through work or effort
example: He earns a comfortable salary at the firm.

meaning_en: Relating to the economy or finances
example: Economic reforms boosted growth significantly.

meaning_en: Using money or resources carefully
example: She is very economical with household expenses.

meaning_en: Result or outcome (noun)
example: The effect of the medicine was immediate.

meaning_en: To draw out a response or reaction
example: The question elicited an honest answer.

meaning_en: Qualified or suitable for something
example: He is eligible for the scholarship.

meaning_en: To escape or avoid cleverly
example: The thief eluded the police for weeks.

meaning_en: Famous or distinguished in a field
example: He is an eminent scientist in the country.

meaning_en: Able to understand and share another's feelings
example: A good counsellor must be empathetic to clients.

meaning_en: To make certain that something happens
example: Ensure all windows are locked before leaving.

meaning_en: Wishing you had what another person has
example: She was envious of his success abroad.

meaning_en: Excluding
example: Everyone attended except Rahul.

meaning_en: Stated clearly and in detail
example: Please give explicit instructions to avoid confusion.

meaning_en: Greater physical distance
example: Jaipur is farther from Delhi than Agra.

meaning_en: Smaller number of countable things
example: Fewer students passed this year than last.

meaning_en: To display something proudly or ostentatiously
example: He flaunts his wealth at every occasion.

meaning_en: To openly disregard a rule or convention
example: He flouted traffic rules repeatedly.

meaning_en: The first of two things mentioned
example: Of tea and coffee, I prefer the former.

meaning_en: Additional or to a greater extent
example: The matter needs further investigation.

meaning_en: A person's manner of walking
example: The soldier had a confident, upright gait.

meaning_en: To bet money on an uncertain outcome
example: He likes to gamble at card games.

meaning_en: To jump or skip about playfully
example: Lambs gambol in the fields during spring.

meaning_en: A hinged barrier or entrance
example: Please close the gate when you leave.

meaning_en: Courteous and kind in manner
example: She was gracious in accepting her defeat.

meaning_en: Feeling or showing thankfulness
example: He was grateful for all the support received.

meaning_en: Causing horror or disgust
example: The police found a grisly scene at the site.

meaning_en: A large species of bear found in North America
example: A grizzly bear can weigh over 400 kg.

meaning_en: Executed by hanging (used only for people)
example: The convicted murderer was hanged at dawn.

meaning_en: Famous or important in history
example: The signing of the treaty was a historic moment.

meaning_en: Relating to history or past events
example: The museum has several historical artefacts.

meaning_en: To accumulate and store secretly
example: He hoards old newspapers in his basement.

meaning_en: A large disorganised crowd or group
example: A horde of protesters gathered outside.

meaning_en: A person; relating to people
example: Humans are the most adaptable species.

meaning_en: Showing compassion and kindness
example: The organisation advocates for humane treatment of animals.

meaning_en: Suspended from above (used for objects)
example: She hung the painting on the drawing room wall.

meaning_en: Impossible to read due to poor handwriting
example: His handwriting is completely illegible.

meaning_en: Forbidden by law or custom
example: Illicit drug trade was banned by the government.

meaning_en: Existing or operating within something
example: The philosopher believed God is immanent in nature.

meaning_en: About to happen very soon
example: A storm is imminent along the coastline.

meaning_en: Suggested but not directly stated
example: There was an implicit understanding between them.

meaning_en: Clever and inventive
example: The engineer found an ingenious solution to the problem.

meaning_en: Innocent, frank, and unsuspecting
example: An ingenuous child asked an awkward question.

meaning_en: To provide financial insurance
example: You must insure your car before driving it.

meaning_en: Fearful of losing what one already has
example: He became jealous when she spoke to others.

meaning_en: Relating to courts of law and judges
example: The judicial system must remain independent.

meaning_en: Having or showing good judgement
example: A judicious use of resources prevents waste.

meaning_en: Central or essential part; a seed
example: The kernel of his argument was sound.

meaning_en: At a subsequent or future time
example: I will call you later in the evening.

meaning_en: The second of two things mentioned
example: Between iron and steel, the latter is stronger.

meaning_en: To put something down — needs an object
example: Lay the books on the table carefully.

meaning_en: To give something temporarily
example: Can you lend me your pen for a moment?

meaning_en: Smaller amount of uncountable things
example: He has less time to complete the paper.

meaning_en: A written false statement that damages reputation
example: The journalist was sued for libel.

meaning_en: To recline — needs no object
example: Lie down and rest for a while.

meaning_en: Not tight or firmly fixed
example: This shirt is very loose around the waist.

meaning_en: To be unable to find or to fail to win
example: Do not lose your admit card before the exam.

meaning_en: An award given for achievement
example: She won a gold medal at the Olympics.

meaning_en: To interfere in others' affairs
example: Do not meddle in matters that do not concern you.

meaning_en: A device that measures and records
example: The electricity meter was checked by the officer.

meaning_en: A unit of length equal to 100 cm
example: The cloth was exactly two metres long.

meaning_en: To have a strong effect against something
example: His past record militated against his promotion.

meaning_en: To make something less severe or serious
example: Rest and medicine mitigate the symptoms.

meaning_en: Relating to right and wrong behaviour
example: Moral values should be taught from childhood.

meaning_en: Confidence and spirit of a group
example: The team's morale was high after the victory.

meaning_en: Causing nausea in others
example: The smell of the garbage was nauseating.

meaning_en: Feeling sick or queasy
example: She felt nauseous throughout the journey.

meaning_en: Relating to a country's navy
example: The naval base was established on the coast.

meaning_en: The small hollow on the abdomen
example: The scar was located near her navel.

meaning_en: Famous or worthy of attention
example: He is a notable figure in Indian politics.

meaning_en: Easily observed or detected
example: There was a noticeable improvement in her work.

meaning_en: A long pole used for rowing a boat
example: He pulled hard on the oar against the current.

meaning_en: Becoming out of date gradually
example: This method is obsolescent and being phased out.

meaning_en: No longer in use or production
example: That technology is now completely obsolete.

meaning_en: Spoken by mouth specifically
example: The oral examination was held in the morning.

meaning_en: A naturally occurring mineral containing metal
example: Iron ore is found in abundance in Jharkhand.

meaning_en: Located on the outside
example: The outer wall of the fort was very thick.

meaning_en: To fail to notice something
example: He overlooked the error in the final report.

meaning_en: To supervise and manage work
example: She was appointed to oversee the entire project.

meaning_en: Private or belonging to an individual
example: He kept his personal life separate from work.

meaning_en: The staff or employees of an organisation
example: All army personnel were present at the parade.

meaning_en: Simple, or a flat area of land
example: She wore a plain white saree to the function.

meaning_en: An aircraft or a flat geometric surface
example: The plane landed safely at the airport.

meaning_en: Able to be done or put into practice
example: The proposed solution is not practicable within budget.

meaning_en: Concerned with actual use rather than theory
example: She gave practical advice on time management.

meaning_en: To recommend or officially order something
example: The doctor prescribed complete bed rest.

meaning_en: Most important, or the head of a school
example: The school principal addressed the students.

meaning_en: A fundamental rule or belief
example: He stood by his principles even under pressure.

meaning_en: To officially forbid something
example: The organisation was proscribed under anti-terror laws.

meaning_en: To reject or suppress officially
example: The High Court quashed the lower court order.

meaning_en: Making little or no noise
example: Please keep quiet in the examination hall.

meaning_en: Completely or to a considerable degree
example: The answer you gave is quite correct.

meaning_en: To lift something up — needs an object
example: Raise your hand if you know the answer.

meaning_en: Unwilling to do something
example: He was reluctant to accept the new assignment.

meaning_en: Showing politeness and respect
example: Students should maintain a respectful tone in class.

meaning_en: Belonging separately to each person mentioned
example: They returned to their respective classrooms.

meaning_en: Not revealing thoughts or feelings readily
example: She is reticent about discussing her personal life.

meaning_en: Correct, or the direction opposite to left
example: You are absolutely right in your analysis.

meaning_en: To move upward by itself — no object needed
example: The sun will rise at six tomorrow morning.

meaning_en: A religious or ceremonial act
example: The last rite was performed at the cremation ground.

meaning_en: Showing practical good sense
example: She made a sensible decision under pressure.

meaning_en: Easily affected by emotions or stimuli
example: This is a sensitive topic and must be handled carefully.

meaning_en: To join fabric using needle and thread
example: She can sew her own clothes from scratch.

meaning_en: A spoken false statement that harms reputation
example: He was taken to court for slander.

meaning_en: Therefore, or to that extent
example: I was exhausted, so I went to sleep early.

meaning_en: To plant seeds in the ground
example: Farmers sow seeds at the onset of monsoon.

meaning_en: To crush or squeeze something flat
example: He squashed the insect under his shoe.

meaning_en: Not moving
example: The vehicle remained stationary at the signal.

meaning_en: Writing and office materials
example: Buy notebooks and pens from the stationery shop.

meaning_en: A set of clothes, or to be convenient
example: He wore a formal black suit to the interview.

meaning_en: A set of connected rooms or musical pieces
example: They booked the presidential suite at the hotel.

meaning_en: Feeling pity or sorrow for another's misfortune
example: She was sympathetic towards the flood victims.

meaning_en: Used in comparisons
example: She scored higher marks than I did.

meaning_en: Belonging to them
example: Their house is located near the railway station.

meaning_en: At that time, or next in sequence
example: He finished the paper, then left the hall.

meaning_en: In, at, or to that place
example: Please sit there and wait for your turn.

meaning_en: Contraction of they are
example: They're ready to begin the presentation now.

meaning_en: Complete and careful in every detail
example: The police conducted a thorough investigation.

meaning_en: Past tense of throw
example: He threw the ball across the entire field.

meaning_en: From one end or side to the other
example: He drove through the tunnel without stopping.

meaning_en: Cloudy or muddy (liquid)
example: The river water was turbid after heavy rainfall.

meaning_en: Swollen or pompously dull in style
example: His turgid prose made the essay unreadable.

meaning_en: Not having knowledge of something
example: She was unaware of the change in schedule.

meaning_en: Unexpectedly or by surprise
example: The announcement caught everyone unawares.

meaning_en: Having no interest or curiosity
example: He seemed uninterested in the entire discussion.

meaning_en: Relating to a city or town
example: Urban infrastructure needs better maintenance.

meaning_en: Suave, polite and refined in manner
example: He is an urbane and charming diplomat.

meaning_en: Complete or absolute, or to speak
example: The entire plan was in utter confusion.

meaning_en: Excessively proud, or producing no result
example: All his vain attempts to escape failed.

meaning_en: A device showing the direction of the wind
example: The weather vane on the roof turned east.

meaning_en: To change or be different
example: Prices of vegetables vary with the season.

meaning_en: A blood vessel that carries blood to the heart
example: The nurse located a vein to draw blood.

meaning_en: Willing to act dishonestly for money
example: The venal officer was caught accepting bribes.

meaning_en: A minor sin or fault that can be forgiven
example: It was a venial mistake, easily overlooked.

meaning_en: Relating to words, spoken or written
example: A verbal agreement was made between the parties.

meaning_en: To a high degree or extent
example: She was very pleased with the result.

meaning_en: The part of the body between ribs and hips
example: She wore a belt tightly around her waist.

meaning_en: To use carelessly or squander
example: Do not waste water during a drought.

meaning_en: Lacking physical or mental strength
example: He felt weak after the long illness.

meaning_en: Atmospheric conditions at a particular time
example: The weather was pleasant during the morning walk.

meaning_en: A period of seven consecutive days
example: The results will be declared in one week.

meaning_en: Expressing doubt or introducing a condition
example: Ask whether she is attending the meeting.

meaning_en: Contraction of who is or who has
example: Who's responsible for this error?

meaning_en: Belonging to or associated with whom
example: Whose bag is lying on the bench?

meaning_en: To form letters or words on a surface
example: Write your full name on the answer sheet.

meaning_en: Relating to hospitality shown to guests
example: The xenial host made all visitors feel welcome.

meaning_en: Intense fear or dislike of foreigners
example: Xenophobia is a serious social problem globally.

meaning_en: To have a strong longing for something
example: She yearns to travel abroad someday.

meaning_en: A wooden frame joining two animals, or a burden
example: The oxen pulled the plough under the yoke.

meaning_en: The yellow centre of an egg
example: Separate the yolk from the white carefully.

meaning_en: Great energy and enthusiasm for a cause
example: She works with remarkable zeal and dedication.

meaning_en: Excessive and fanatical enthusiasm
example: Religious zealotry has caused many conflicts in history.

meaning_en: A defined area for a particular purpose
example: No vehicles are allowed in the pedestrian zone.
